video_subject: Forex Market: Dollar Hits New High as Euro Slides
video_script: The US dollar just hit its strongest level since mid-May 2025, and the euro is feeling the pressure. EUR/USD slipped below 1.15 today, its lowest since late March, after the Federal Reserve held rates steady but flagged something traders weren't expecting: nine policymakers now see a hike before the end of 2026. That hawkish signal is reshaping the whole currency map. The European Central Bank already raised rates last week, and the Bank of Japan followed just days later, yet neither move was enough to offset the dollar's pull as investors chase higher US yields. Adding to the mix, a reported US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz pushed oil to three-month lows, easing inflation worries but doing nothing to cool the dollar rally. For now, analysts see EUR/USD drifting toward 1.17 this month before sliding back below 1.14 by year-end. The next move depends on how far the Fed is really willing to go.
